scenes.bottom_row_text: Negative Air Incompatible With Flex Ducts
scenes.notes: Cold open monologue — host introduces the episode, the report, and the guest before David Hart responds.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Negative Air Incompatible With Flex Ducts
scenes.notes: Hart confirms the report is making waves; host asks for the gist — two-person exchange.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Negative Air Incompatible With Flex Ducts
scenes.notes: Hart explains the report title and how negative air was designed for metal ducts, not flex — b-roll of duct illustrates the system being discussed.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Introducing Guest John Miles & His Key Insight
scenes.notes: Hart introduces John Miles, recounts the pivotal meeting with Superstratum where Miles said flex duct was never meant to be under negative pressure.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Introducing Guest John Miles & His Key Insight
scenes.notes: Hart finishes intro of Miles, hands over to him — all three on screen as Miles is brought into the conversation.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Flex Duct Adhesive: High Shear, Low Peel Strength
scenes.notes: Miles delivers a sustained monologue on his 35-year career and the adhesive science behind flex duct — single camera on the expert.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Flex Duct Adhesive: High Shear, Low Peel Strength
scenes.notes: Host summarises the flap/peel concept and asks Miles to confirm — all three engaged; b-roll of duct reinforces the structural point.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Flex Duct Adhesive: High Shear, Low Peel Strength
scenes.notes: Miles explains the 20:1 pressure ratio from manufacturer specs — three-way conversation continues.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Shear vs. Peel: Why Negative Pressure Destroys Flex Duct
scenes.notes: Hart clarifies shear vs. peel strength analogy (Post-it note), explains the 10–20x positive vs. negative pressure tolerance — three-way dialogue with Miles confirming.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Shear vs. Peel: Why Negative Pressure Destroys Flex Duct
scenes.notes: Miles explains why the polymer was engineered for oil resistance and shear strength, not peel — all three engaged.

scenes.bottom_row_text: What Is Negative Air Duct Cleaning?
scenes.notes: Host pivots to define negative air duct cleaning for the audience; Hart and Miles both contribute — topic shift warrants new heading.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Structural Damage Caused by Negative Air Cleaning
scenes.notes: Miles details delamination, wire deformation, and the snowball effect of damage — b-roll of duct illustrates structural damage; Hart adds the snowball analogy.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Structural Damage Caused by Negative Air Cleaning
scenes.notes: Miles explains how unconditioned air infiltration leads to condensation, mold growth, and contamination spread — three-way discussion continues.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Mold Growth After Negative Air Cleaning
scenes.notes: Hart asks Miles about mold growth observed after negative air cleaning; Miles confirms the pattern from his chemistry business — topic shifts to mold consequences.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Mold Growth After Negative Air Cleaning
scenes.notes: Miles explains the Superstratum solution pairing with positive pressure cleaning to prevent re-contamination — three-way exchange.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Hidden Damage You Can't See or Detect
scenes.notes: Host raises the point that duct damage is invisible to homeowners; Hart uses the lung cancer analogy — topic shifts to hidden/undetectable damage.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Hidden Damage You Can't See or Detect
scenes.notes: Hart describes the NASA/DoD-level laboratory used for the research and the delamination images — b-roll of Hart reinforces credibility of the speaker; Miles concurs.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Why Mitigation Steps Are Impractical & Asinine
scenes.notes: Host asks about mitigation options; Hart begins laying out the impractical steps required to safely use negative air on flex duct — new topic heading.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Why Mitigation Steps Are Impractical & Asinine
scenes.notes: Hart delivers an extended monologue detailing the three impossible mitigation steps and the NADCA instructor anecdote — single camera on Hart as he holds the floor.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Positive Air Cleaning: The Right Method
scenes.notes: Host asks whether the paper tested positive pressure cleaning; Hart explains why positive pressure tolerance is so high it was not the focus — topic shifts to positive air.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Positive Air Cleaning: The Right Method
scenes.notes: Hart explains using the air handler in positive pressure cleaning, training programs, and the damper/debris problem with negative air — two-person exchange.

scenes.bottom_row_text: SantaJet & Superstratum: Decontamination Done Right
scenes.notes: Host introduces the SantaJet and Superstratum pairing — b-roll of Superstratum product illustrates the chemistry being discussed; topic shifts to decontamination method.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: SantaJet & Superstratum: Decontamination Done Right
scenes.notes: Hart explains why fogging fails (through vs. onto) and how SantaJet delivers 100% surface coverage — all three engaged as Miles and host react.

scenes.bottom_row_text: SantaJet & Superstratum: Decontamination Done Right
scenes.notes: Miles clarifies the decontaminant vs. disinfectant language and explains why saturation fogging wastes chemistry and stresses adhesives — three-way exchange.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Industry Standards: No Differentiation Yet
scenes.notes: Host pivots to ask about industry standards and whether the industry differentiates between negative and positive air cleaning — topic shifts to industry response.

scenes.top_row_text: David Hart | Founder & CEO, RAM Air International
scenes.bottom_row_text: Industry Standards: No Differentiation Yet
scenes.notes: Hart explains positive air cleaning is in 24 countries but traditional method still dominates; host asks if industry has addressed it — three-way discussion.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Industry Standards: No Differentiation Yet
scenes.notes: Miles argues the industry is embarrassed — 40 years of specs showed positive was stronger but no one acted; Hart adds the cigarette analogy — three-way close of topic.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Closing: A Seismic Shift in the Industry
scenes.notes: Host wraps up, thanks guests, invites final thoughts — Hart declines final thoughts humorously; host delivers closing sign-off.

scenes.bottom_row_text: Closing: A Seismic Shift in the Industry
scenes.notes: Host delivers clean second-take closing with 'seismic shift' line; Hart and Miles sign off — use this take as the episode outro.
